title: garnotia patula
intro: Garnotia patula, also known as the East Indian hawthorn, is a type of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Garnotia patula include its ability to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Garnotia patula can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
